nodes.label: Unattended overnight execution
nodes.description: The pipeline must run start-to-finish without human intervention, completing before the user wakes up.

nodes.label: Surface relevant job listings
nodes.description: Filter scraped listings to only those matching the user's skills, seniority, and role preferences.

nodes.label: Generate tailored resume PDFs
nodes.description: For each relevant listing, produce a resume PDF customized to that job's requirements.

nodes.label: Scrape multiple job boards
nodes.description: Collect listings from both standard and auth-protected platforms like LinkedIn and Wellfound.

nodes.label: Prepare materials for morning review
nodes.description: Output is a shortlist of relevant jobs with tailored resumes, ready for human review — not auto-apply.

nodes.label: Cross-run deduplication
nodes.description: The database must not store duplicate job listings across pipeline runs.

nodes.label: Auth-protected scraping
nodes.description: Must handle login-gated platforms like LinkedIn and Wellfound.

nodes.label: Dynamic page handling
nodes.description: Must handle JS-rendered content, infinite scroll, and lazy-loaded listings.

nodes.label: Bot detection evasion
nodes.description: Must employ stealth techniques to avoid being blocked by anti-bot systems.

nodes.label: Full error recovery
nodes.description: Pipeline must recover from transient failures without human intervention.

nodes.label: Offline test harness
nodes.description: Testing must validate scraping logic without making live HTTP calls.

nodes.label: Persistent storage
nodes.description: Scraped listings must persist across runs in a database.

nodes.label: Profile-based filtering
nodes.description: Listings must be scored/filtered against a user-defined profile.

nodes.label: Use Python as runtime
nodes.description: Python chosen as the primary language for the entire pipeline.
nodes.rationale: Best LLM SDK ecosystem, mature Playwright bindings, WeasyPrint for PDF, SQLAlchemy for ORM, pytest for testing.

nodes.label: Use SQLite via SQLAlchemy
nodes.description: SQLite as the persistent store, accessed through SQLAlchemy ORM.
nodes.rationale: Single-machine pipeline, hundreds of listings, no server to manage, portable file-based DB.

nodes.label: Use Playwright for browser automation
nodes.description: Playwright chosen for all scraping with stealth plugin.
nodes.rationale: Modern async API, built-in auto-wait, playwright-stealth for anti-bot evasion.

nodes.label: LLM-driven resume tailoring
nodes.description: Use an LLM API to rewrite resume sections per job.
nodes.rationale: Dynamic content generation matching job descriptions.

nodes.label: HTML/CSS to PDF via WeasyPrint
nodes.description: Render tailored content to PDF using WeasyPrint.
nodes.rationale: No TeX dependency, flexible styling, Docker-friendly.

nodes.label: Local-first with Docker portability
nodes.description: Run locally via cron, with Dockerfile for portability.
nodes.rationale: Start simple, containerize for deployment flexibility.

nodes.label: Site-specific scraper adapters
nodes.description: Each job board gets a dedicated adapter class implementing a common interface.
nodes.rationale: Job boards have wildly different DOM structures and auth flows. A shared interface ensures the pipeline is decoupled from site-specific logic, and new boards are added by writing one adapter.

nodes.label: Stage-based pipeline with checkpointing
nodes.description: Pipeline is divided into discrete stages (scrape, ingest, filter, tailor, report) with state persisted between stages.
nodes.rationale: If the tailor stage fails at job #47, a restart can resume from job #47 instead of re-scraping everything. Each stage writes its output to DB, enabling partial recovery.

nodes.label: Recorded-fixture testing
nodes.description: Tests use saved HTML snapshots and JSON responses instead of live network calls.
nodes.rationale: Meets the offline test harness requirement. Fixtures are captured once from real sites, then replayed deterministically.

nodes.label: Cookie-based auth for protected sites
nodes.description: User provides session cookies (or performs one-time browser login) which are persisted and reused across runs.
nodes.rationale: Automating OAuth/login flows for LinkedIn is fragile and risks account lockout. Reusing cookies from a manual login is more reliable.

nodes.label: Scraper engine
nodes.description: Playwright-based scraper with site-specific adapters for each job board. Handles pagination, dynamic loading, and stealth.

nodes.label: LinkedIn adapter
nodes.description: Scraper adapter for LinkedIn job search. Uses saved cookies for auth, handles infinite-scroll pagination.

nodes.label: Wellfound adapter
nodes.description: Scraper adapter for Wellfound (AngelList). Handles React-rendered listings and auth.

nodes.label: Generic board adapter
nodes.description: Config-driven adapter for simpler job boards using CSS selectors defined in YAML.

nodes.label: Database store
nodes.description: SQLAlchemy models and CRUD operations over SQLite. Handles deduplication via composite unique keys (url + source).

nodes.label: Relevance filter
nodes.description: Scores each listing against the user profile using keyword matching, seniority detection, and optional LLM-based semantic matching.

nodes.label: Resume tailor
nodes.description: Sends user's base resume + job description to LLM API, receives tailored resume content structured as JSON.

nodes.label: PDF renderer
nodes.description: Takes tailored resume JSON, fills an HTML/CSS template, and renders to PDF via WeasyPrint.

nodes.label: Pipeline orchestrator
nodes.description: Coordinates all stages in sequence with retry logic, checkpointing, and structured logging. Entry point for overnight runs.

nodes.label: Morning report generator
nodes.description: Produces a summary HTML/Markdown report of all relevant jobs found, with links to tailored resume PDFs.

nodes.label: Configuration system
nodes.description: YAML-based config for user profile, job board settings, and credentials. Env vars for secrets.

nodes.label: Test harness
nodes.description: pytest-based test suite using recorded HTML fixtures and mocked LLM responses.

nodes.label: Normalized job listing schema
nodes.description: Common data contract all scraper adapters must output, regardless of source site.
nodes.contract: { title: str, company: str, url: str, source: str, location: str, description: str, tags: list[str], scraped_at: datetime, raw_html: str | None }

nodes.label: Scraper adapter interface
nodes.description: Abstract base class that all site-specific adapters must implement.
nodes.contract: class BaseAdapter: async def scrape(self, config) -> list[JobListing]; async def login(self, credentials) -> None

nodes.label: User profile schema
nodes.description: YAML schema defining the user's skills, experience, role preferences, and base resume content.
nodes.contract: { name, email, skills: list, seniority: str, roles: list, locations: list, base_resume: { summary, experience: list, education: list, projects: list } }

nodes.label: LLM API interface
nodes.description: Abstraction over LLM provider (OpenAI/Anthropic) for resume tailoring calls.
nodes.contract: async def tailor_resume(base_resume: dict, job: JobListing, model: str) -> TailoredResume

nodes.label: Pipeline stage interface
nodes.description: Each pipeline stage implements a common run/resume protocol for checkpointing.
nodes.contract: class Stage: async def run(self, context: PipelineContext) -> StageResult; def can_resume(self) -> bool

nodes.label: playwright-stealth suffices for bot evasion
nodes.description: Assumes the playwright-stealth plugin is enough to bypass bot detection on LinkedIn, Wellfound, etc. May need residential proxies or additional fingerprint randomization.

nodes.label: LLM produces usable resume content
nodes.description: Assumes GPT-4 or Claude can generate professional, accurate, non-hallucinated resume rewrites given a structured prompt and base resume.

nodes.label: Job board DOM is reasonably stable
nodes.description: Assumes that target job boards do not change their HTML structure frequently enough to break adapters between maintenance cycles.

nodes.label: SQLite handles expected volume
nodes.description: Assumes nightly scraping produces hundreds (not millions) of listings, well within SQLite's capability.

nodes.label: WeasyPrint produces professional PDFs
nodes.description: Assumes WeasyPrint's CSS rendering is sufficient for a professional-looking resume. Some advanced CSS (flexbox) may not render perfectly.

nodes.label: Session cookies last through overnight run
nodes.description: Assumes that saved session cookies for LinkedIn/Wellfound remain valid for the 2-4 hour duration of an overnight run.

nodes.label: LLM API is available and affordable
nodes.description: Assumes OpenAI/Anthropic API is reachable at runtime and cost per run (dozens of tailoring calls) is acceptable.

nodes.label: Local cron/launchd triggers reliably
nodes.description: Assumes the machine is awake or scheduled to wake for the cron job. macOS may sleep and skip cron triggers.

nodes.label: Bot detection breaks scraping
nodes.description: Job boards may update bot detection (CAPTCHAs, fingerprinting) at any time, silently breaking scraping. LinkedIn is particularly aggressive.

nodes.label: DOM structure changes break adapters
nodes.description: Any HTML restructuring by a job board will break the corresponding adapter's selectors, requiring manual maintenance.

nodes.label: LLM hallucinates resume content
nodes.description: The LLM might fabricate skills, experience, or credentials not present in the base resume, producing misleading applications.

nodes.label: Auth cookies expire mid-run
nodes.description: Session tokens may expire or be invalidated during the pipeline run, causing auth-protected scraping to fail partway.

nodes.label: Rate limiting by job boards
nodes.description: Aggressive scraping may trigger rate limits or IP bans, especially on LinkedIn.

nodes.label: Terms of Service violations
nodes.description: Scraping LinkedIn and Wellfound may violate their ToS. Legal risk, though enforcement against individuals is rare.

nodes.label: PDF formatting issues
nodes.description: Complex resume layouts may not render correctly in WeasyPrint, especially multi-column designs or custom fonts.

nodes.label: Unexpected LLM API costs
nodes.description: If many jobs match the filter, dozens of LLM calls per night could accumulate significant costs without a budget cap.

nodes.label: macOS sleeps through cron job
nodes.description: If running locally on macOS, the machine may be asleep when the cron job is scheduled, causing it to never execute.

edges.rationale: Playwright stealth may not keep pace with evolving bot detection.
Adapters become stale when site DOM changes.
